Electrode.description: Specifies electrical boundary conditions for a device contact, including voltage, current, or charge boundary conditions, as well as Schottky and resistive contact models.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the electrode, or Regexp(<pattern>) for regular expression matching.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Contact voltage in V. Can also use (val1 At val2, ...) for transient piecewise-linear voltage.

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Current boundary condition in A/um/d. Voltage is used as initial guess only.

parameters.name: Charge
parameters.desc: Charge for floating electrode in C. Voltage must not be specified.

parameters.name: Barrier
parameters.desc: Barrier voltage in V.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.desc: Electrode material for contacts on insulators. Can include doping spec (N=<val> or P=<val>).

eSHEDistributionPlot.description: Plots the electron-energy distribution at specified spatial positions for the Spherical Harmonics Expansion Method (SHE). Used to visualize hot-carrier distribution.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate the electron distribution function.

CurrentPlot.description: Specifies which current-related quantities to write to the current plot (.plt) file. Controls output of terminal currents, contact currents, and integrated quantities during simulation.

Math.description: Specifies numerical methods and solver settings, including equation discretization, linear solver selection, convergence criteria, and error control. Can be restricted to a specific location.

parameters.name: Extrapolate
parameters.desc: Use extrapolation for faster convergence.

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method (e.g., Blocked, ILS, Pardiso, Super).

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Relative error target for Newton solver.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of Newton iterations.

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Global error control tolerance.

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error value for convergence check.

OpticalDevice.description: Defines an optical device specification for mixed-mode simulation and Composite Method. Uses the same sub-sections as Device (Table 205).

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Optical device name.

parameters.name: File
parameters.desc: Input and output file specifications.

parameters.name: Electrode
parameters.desc: Electrode boundary conditions.

parameters.name: Physics
parameters.desc: Physical models.

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Plot data specifications.

hSHEDistributionPlot.description: Plots the hole-energy distribution at specified spatial positions for the Spherical Harmonics Expansion Method (SHE). Used to visualize hot-carrier distribution for holes.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate the hole distribution function.

HydrogenBoundary.description: Specifies boundary conditions for hydrogen species transport in degradation models. Each species can have a fixed boundary concentration or a reflective (homogeneous Neumann) condition.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the hydrogen contact.

parameters.name: HydrogenAtom
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenIon
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenMolecule
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesA
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

parameters.name: HydrogenSpeciesB
parameters.desc: Boundary concentration in cm^-3, or 'reflective' for Neumann BC.

FarFieldPlot.description: Generates far-field radiation pattern plots for optical simulations, typically used in LED or VCSEL device simulations to visualize angular emission characteristics.

Plot.description: Specifies which datasets to include in the output TDR plot file. Supports solution variables (e.g., ElectricField, eDensity, Potential), optical quantities, trap distributions, hydrogen species, and derived quantities. The output file is named via the File section's Plot keyword.

File.description: Specifies input and output file names. Global File sections use (g) keywords; device-specific File sections use (d) keywords. Supports grid files, parameter files, plot files, and many specialized output types.

parameters.name: Grid
parameters.desc: (d) Device geometry and mesh file (input .tdr, or @tdr@).

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Spatially distributed simulation results (output .tdr, @tdrdat@).

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Device currents, voltages, charges, temperatures (output .plt, @plot@).

parameters.name: Output
parameters.desc: (g) Runtime log file (output .log, @log@). Default is 'output'.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.desc: (d) Device parameter file (input .par, @parameter@).

parameters.name: Save
parameters.desc: Simulation results for retrieval with Load (output .sav).

File.example:
File {
  Grid="nmos.tdr"
  Parameter="nmos.par"
  Plot="nmos_des"
  Current="nmos_plt"
  Output="nmos_log"
}

BandstructurePlot.description: Plots local band structure data in an LED simulation. Output files use the base name specified in File { Bandstructure=... } and are appended with vertex coordinates and file extensions.

Thermode.description: Specifies thermal boundary conditions for lattice temperature at a contact. Supports fixed temperature, heat flux, and thermal contact resistance/conductance models.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the thermode, or Regexp(<pattern>) for regular expression matching.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Contact temperature in K.

parameters.name: Power
parameters.desc: Heat flux boundary condition in W/cm^2.

parameters.name: SurfaceConductance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal conductance in W/(cm^2*K).

parameters.name: SurfaceResistance
parameters.desc: Contact thermal resistivity in (cm^2*K)/W.

parameters.name: AreaFactor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for heat fluxes (default 1).

Solve.description: Defines the simulation sequence and what equations to solve. Supports coupled Newton, Gummel (Plugin), quasistationary ramps, transient analysis, small-signal AC, harmonic balance, and continuation methods.

parameters.name: Coupled
parameters.desc: Solve coupled equations consistently by Newton iteration.

parameters.name: Quasistationary
parameters.desc: Quasistationary ramp with step control and goal specification.

parameters.name: Transient
parameters.desc: Transient simulation with time-step control.

parameters.name: ACCoupled
parameters.desc: Small-signal AC analysis with frequency sweep.

parameters.name: Plugin
parameters.desc: Solve equations consistently by Gummel iteration.

parameters.name: Continuation
parameters.desc: Continuation method for tracing IV curves beyond breakdown.

VCSELNearFieldPlot.description: Generates near-field optical intensity plots for VCSEL (Vertical-Cavity Surface-Emitting Laser) device simulations.

MonteCarlo.description: Configures the Monte Carlo particle transport simulation. See the Sentaurus Device Monte Carlo User Guide for detailed options.

parameters.name: <options>
parameters.desc: Monte Carlo simulation options as described in the MC User Guide.

Device.description: Defines a device for single-device or mixed-mode simulation. Contains all device-specific sections including electrodes, physics models, plots, math settings, and file I/O.

parameters.name: <string>
parameters.desc: Device name used in mixed-mode System section.

parameters.name: File
parameters.desc: Input and output file specifications (Table 207).

parameters.name: Electrode
parameters.desc: Electrical boundary conditions (Table 206).

parameters.name: Physics
parameters.desc: Physical models and material parameters (Table 236).

parameters.name: Plot
parameters.desc: Output plot datasets (Table 334).

parameters.name: Math
parameters.desc: Numerical solver settings (Table 211).

System.description: Defines the circuit (mixed-mode) section of the command file. Instantiates device models, connects electrodes to circuit nodes, specifies netlists, and controls plotting of circuit quantities.

NonLocalPlot.description: Plots data defined on nonlocal line meshes, including eigenenergies, wavefunctions, and overlap integrals from 1D Schrodinger equation solutions.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates defining the nonlocal evaluation path.

parameters.name: EigenEnergy
parameters.desc: Plot eigenenergies from Schrodinger solver.

parameters.name: WaveFunction
parameters.desc: Plot wavefunctions (with optional Electron/Hole and Number restrictions).

parameters.name: OverlapIntegral
parameters.desc: Plot overlap integrals from Schrodinger solver.

TensorPlot.description: Plots tensor data on the native tensor grid for the beam propagation method (BPM). Used to visualize complex refractive index, optical field, and optical intensity distributions.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the tensor plot, appended to the base file name from File section.

parameters.name: Xmin/Xmax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum x-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: Ymin/Ymax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum y-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: Zmin/Zmax
parameters.desc: Minimum/maximum z-coordinate range in um.

parameters.name: OutputLevel
parameters.desc: Set to 'maximum' to generate plots after every iteration for bidirectional BPM.

GainPlot.description: Configures parameters for gain spectrum plots in LED simulations, including the energy range and number of data points for each gain curve.

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of points to plot for each gain curve.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Energy range in eV as (Emin, Emax), or 'Auto' for automatic range detection.

Physics.description: Specifies physical models for the device simulation, including mobility, recombination, quantum corrections, thermodynamic models, and more. Can be restricted to a specific location (material, region, interface, or electrode).

parameters.name: Mobility
parameters.desc: Mobility model selection (Table 278).

parameters.name: Recombination
parameters.desc: Generation-recombination models (SRH, Auger, Avalanche, etc.).

parameters.name: Fermi
parameters.desc: Enable Fermi-Dirac statistics.

parameters.name: Temperature
parameters.desc: Device (lattice) temperature in K (default 300).

parameters.name: EffectiveIntrinsicDensity
parameters.desc: Band gap and bandgap narrowing model.

parameters.name: Thermodynamic
parameters.desc: Enable thermodynamic transport model.

TrappedCarDistrPlot.description: Plots the trapped carrier charge distribution at a specified position or over a specified location (material, region, etc.). Used to visualize trap occupancy and charge density.

parameters.name: <vector>
parameters.desc: Spatial coordinates (x y z) at which to evaluate trapped carrier charge.

parameters.name: Location
parameters.desc: Restrict the plot to a location (Table 362): Material, Region, MaterialInterface, RegionInterface, Everywhere, Semiconductor, Insulator, Window.

RayTraceBC.description: Section keyword for defining raytracing boundary conditions at special contacts. These contacts are drawn like electrodes and thermodes. Supports Fresnel reflection, TMM multilayer structures, custom PMI models, and configurable reflectivity/transmittivity.

NoisePlot.description: Specifies noise output data to plot, including scalar/vector datasets, local noise sources, local noise voltage spectral densities, and Green's functions.

parameters.name: AllLNS
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise sources.

parameters.name: AllLNVSD
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise voltage spectral densities.

parameters.name: AllLNVXVSD
parameters.desc: Plot all used local noise voltage cross-correlation spectral densities.

parameters.name: GreenFunctions
parameters.desc: Plot all used Green's functions and their gradients.

PlotGainPara.description: Plots gain parameters in an LED or laser simulation, providing modal gain and material gain data for analysis.

PlotFarField.description: Plots the optical far-field radiation pattern of an LED device during a Quasistationary or Transient simulation. Outputs angular-dependent intensity data to the plot file. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345). Requires LED radiation physics to be enabled in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient (range, intervals, decade)

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

BreakCriteria.description: Specifies conditions for prematurely terminating a simulation when monitored values exceed given limits. Useful for stopping breakdown simulations when current exceeds a threshold, or nonisothermal simulations when lattice temperature approaches the melting point. Can be specified globally in the Math section or as a sweep-specific option inside Quasistationary, Transient, or Continuation.

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Contact voltage break criterion: Voltage(Contact="<name>" AbsVal=<float>)

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Contact current break criterion: Current(Contact="<name>" MinVal=<float> MaxVal=<float>)

parameters.name: LatticeTemperature
parameters.desc: Lattice temperature criterion: LatticeTemperature(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: CurrentDensity
parameters.desc: Current density criterion: CurrentDensity(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: ElectricField
parameters.desc: Electric field criterion: ElectricField(maxval=<float>)

parameters.name: DevicePower
parameters.desc: Device power criterion: DevicePower(AbsVal=<float>)

Isource_pset.description: Declares a SPICE current source instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The current source injects a current from node0 to node1. Supports DC, AC, pulse, piecewise-linear, and sinusoidal waveform specifications. The default parameter set Isource_pset provides standard SPICE current source parameters.

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC current value (A)

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC current amplitude for small-signal analysis

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.desc: Pulse waveform: pulse=(i1 i2 td tr tf pw period)

parameters.name: pwl
parameters.desc: Piecewise-linear waveform: pwl=(t1 i1 t2 i2 ...)

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Sinusoidal waveform: sine=(vo va freq td theta phi)

PlotLEDRadiation.description: Plots the optical far-field radiation pattern of an LED device during a Quasistationary or Transient simulation. This is a specialized plot command for LED simulations that outputs the angular-dependent far-field intensity. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345). Requires LED radiation raytracing to be enabled in the Physics section.

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

HB.description: Specifies global harmonic balance (HB) analysis options in the Math section. HB is a frequency-domain method for solving periodic or quasi-periodic time-dependent problems. Controls the analysis mode (MDFT or SDFT), convergence parameters, logarithmic variable transformations, and solve spectrum truncation. MDFT mode supports multitone analysis and requires CMI-compatible compact models.

parameters.name: MDFT
parameters.desc: Enable multidimensional Fourier transform mode for multitone analysis

parameters.name: LogDensity
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic density variables in HB Newton algorithm

parameters.name: LogTemperature
parameters.desc: Use logarithmic temperature variables in HB Newton algorithm

parameters.name: CNormPrint
parameters.desc: Print residuum, update error, and correction count per Newton step

parameters.name: SolveSpectrum
parameters.desc: Define a named solve spectrum for truncation: SolveSpectrum(Name="<name>") { (<h1> <h2>)... }

Window.description: In the Math section, defines a spatial window or region for localized numerical operations such as mesh refinement, plotting, or solver constraints.

VCVS_pset.description: Declares a voltage-controlled voltage source (VCVS) instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The output voltage between out+ and out- is equal to the gain times the voltage difference between in+ and in-. The default parameter set VCVS_pset provides standard SPICE VCVS parameters.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Voltage gain (default: 1)

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC transfer gain value

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC transfer gain for small-signal analysis

PlotBandstructure.description: Plots band structure data in an LED simulation during a Quasistationary or Transient run. Outputs local band structure information including eigenvalues and eigenfunctions at specified vertices. Requires the k.p model or Schrodinger equation to be solved. The base name for output files is set by Bandstructure in the File section. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

Resistor_pset.description: Declares a SPICE resistor instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. Connects between two circuit nodes with a specified resistance. The default parameter set Resistor_pset provides standard SPICE resistor parameters. Widely used as contact resistors, load resistors, and thermal resistors in mixed-mode circuit simulations.

parameters.name: resistance
parameters.desc: Resistance value in ohms (default: 1)

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature

VCCS_pset.description: Declares a voltage-controlled current source (VCCS) instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. The output current from out+ to out- is equal to the transconductance times the voltage difference between in+ and in-. The default parameter set VCCS_pset provides standard SPICE VCCS parameters.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Transconductance gain in siemens (default: 1)

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC transfer value

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC transfer value for small-signal analysis

SpectralPlot.description: Plots spectral simulation results for each entry of the illumination spectrum. Outputs spatially distributed results to a TDR file and spectral curves to a PLT file. Consecutive plots are collected in a single file by default. Used in conjunction with the IlluminationSpectrum option in the Physics section for wavelength-dependent optical simulations. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Write to separate enumerated files

PlotGain.description: Plots stimulated and spontaneous emission spectra data in an LED or laser simulation during a Quasistationary or Transient run. Outputs modal gain and spontaneous emission data. The base name for output files is set by Gain in the File section. Follows the same parameter syntax as the Plot command (Table 345).

parameters.name: FilePrefix
parameters.desc: Prefix of the output file name

parameters.name: Overwrite
parameters.desc: Rewrite the same file name at each loop (default: on)

parameters.name: noOverwrite
parameters.desc: Give each new plot file a new name by numbering

parameters.name: Time
parameters.desc: Time points for plotting during transient

parameters.name: When
parameters.desc: Generate output when a target voltage or current is crossed

Capacitor_pset.description: Declares a SPICE capacitor instance in the System section for mixed-mode simulations. Connects between two circuit nodes with a specified capacitance. The default parameter set Capacitor_pset provides standard SPICE capacitor parameters. Used for coupling capacitors, bypass capacitors, and charge storage elements in mixed-mode circuit simulations.

parameters.name: capacitance
parameters.desc: Capacitance value in farads (default: 1)

parameters.name: ic
parameters.desc: Initial voltage across the capacitor

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient

Continuation.description: Enables automated tracing of arbitrarily shaped I-V curves using a dynamic load-line technique. Particularly useful for tracing multivalued I-V curves associated with breakdown, snapback, or latchup phenomena. The method adapts boundary conditions along the I-V curve by adjusting a variable load resistor attached to the continuation electrode, ensuring the load line remains orthogonal to the local tangent of the I-V curve.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Electrode name for continuation (prefix with <device>. for mixed mode)

parameters.name: InitialVstep
parameters.desc: Initial voltage-controlled step size (V)

parameters.name: MinVoltage
parameters.desc: Minimum voltage limit of the continuation window (V)

parameters.name: MaxVoltage
parameters.desc: Maximum voltage limit of the continuation window (V)

parameters.name: MinCurrent
parameters.desc: Minimum current limit of the continuation window (A)

parameters.name: MaxCurrent
parameters.desc: Maximum current limit of the continuation window (A)

Extraction.description: Specifies voltage-dependent extraction curves to be generated during a Quasistationary ramp. Multiple curves can be listed. No extraction output is produced if the Extraction option is missing from the Quasistationary statement. For AC and noise simulations, frequency-dependent curves are specified within an ACCoupled statement instead.

parameters.name: IdVg
parameters.desc: Drain current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IsVg
parameters.desc: Source current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IgVg
parameters.desc: Gate current vs. gate voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IdVd
parameters.desc: Drain current vs. drain voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: IbVd
parameters.desc: Bulk current vs. drain voltage extraction curve

parameters.name: Ads_Vg
parameters.desc: AC conductance Ads vs. gate voltage (AC analysis)

ACCoupled.description: Performs small-signal AC analysis. Extends a Coupled section with parameters for computing the frequency-dependent admittance matrix between circuit nodes. Typically used in mixed-mode simulations. The admittance matrix Y = A + i*2*pi*f*C is decomposed into conductance (A) and capacitance (C) matrices.

parameters.name: StartFrequency
parameters.desc: Start frequency for AC analysis sweep [Hz]

parameters.name: EndFrequency
parameters.desc: End frequency for AC analysis sweep [Hz]

parameters.name: NumberOfPoints
parameters.desc: Number of frequency points in the sweep

parameters.name: Decade
parameters.desc: Use decade (logarithmic) frequency spacing

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: List of AC circuit nodes for admittance matrix computation

parameters.name: Exclude
parameters.desc: List of circuit instances to exclude from AC analysis

CCVS_pset.description: Defines a current-controlled voltage source (CCVS) compact model instance in the System section. The output voltage is proportional to the current flowing through a specified sensing element. The CCVS follows the relation V = gain * I_sense.

parameters.name: gain
parameters.desc: Transimpedance gain [Ohm], voltage per unit sensing current

parameters.name: tc1
parameters.desc: First-order temperature coefficient for gain

parameters.name: tc2
parameters.desc: Second-order temperature coefficient for gain

parameters.name: ic
parameters.desc: Initial condition (voltage) for transient analysis

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature [C]

SolveSpectrum.description: References a predefined solve spectrum for use in an HBCoupled (harmonic balance) statement. The spectrum is defined in the HB section of the Math section and allows truncating the full harmonic balance spectrum to only specific (multi-)indices, reducing computational cost. Each multi-index corresponds to an intermodulation frequency combination.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Name of the solve spectrum as defined in Math { HB { SolveSpectrum (Name=...) {...} } }

Goal.description: Defines the target values for ramping in a Quasistationary command. Goals can specify contact voltages, currents, charges, quasi-Fermi potentials in doping wells, circuit node voltages, or physical model parameters. Multiple Goal statements can be used to ramp several quantities simultaneously.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.desc: Contact name to ramp (or Regexp pattern for matching multiple contacts)

parameters.name: Voltage
parameters.desc: Target voltage value [V] for the contact

parameters.name: Current
parameters.desc: Target current value [A] for the contact

parameters.name: Node
parameters.desc: Circuit node name to ramp (mixed mode)

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.desc: Circuit model parameter to ramp, e.g. r1."resistance"

parameters.name: Value
parameters.desc: Target value for parameter or node ramping

Diode_pset.description: Defines a diode compact model instance in the System section for mixed-mode circuit simulations. The diode follows the SPICE diode model with parameters for saturation current, series resistance, junction capacitance, transit time, and breakdown characteristics.

parameters.name: is
parameters.desc: Saturation current [A]

parameters.name: rs
parameters.desc: Ohmic series resistance [Ohm]

parameters.name: n
parameters.desc: Emission coefficient (ideality factor)

parameters.name: cjo
parameters.desc: Zero-bias junction capacitance [F]

parameters.name: tt
parameters.desc: Transit time [s]

parameters.name: bv
parameters.desc: Reverse breakdown voltage [V]

Transient.description: Performs a transient time-domain simulation. The device must already have an initial solution. The simulation advances time from InitialTime to FinalTime, re-solving the device equations at each time step. Supports TRBDF (default) and backward Euler (BE) integration methods. Can also be used in a Quasistationary-like mode to ramp boundary conditions with Goal statements.

parameters.name: InitialTime
parameters.desc: Start time of the transient simulation [s]

parameters.name: FinalTime
parameters.desc: End time of the transient simulation [s]

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Initial time step size [s]

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum allowed time step [s]

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum allowed time step [s]

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Multiplier for step size when the previous step converged (default: 2)

DopingWell.description: Specifies a doping well by the coordinates of a point inside it. Used in the Goal and Set statements of a Quasistationary command to ramp electron or hole quasi-Fermi potentials in the specified well. More general than WellContactName because it works for buried wells without contacts. In mixed-mode simulations, the device instance name must be prefixed.

parameters.name: x
parameters.desc: X coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm]

parameters.name: y
parameters.desc: Y coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm]

parameters.name: z
parameters.desc: Z coordinate of a point inside the doping well [cm] (3D only)

HBCoupled.description: Performs harmonic balance (HB) analysis, a frequency-domain method for solving periodic or quasi-periodic time-dependent problems. Supports single-tone (SDFT mode) and multi-tone (MDFT mode) analysis. The Tone option is mandatory. More computationally efficient than transient analysis for problems with widely different time constants.

parameters.name: Tone
parameters.desc: Base frequency and number of harmonics, e.g. Tone(Frequency=1e9 NumberOfHarmonics=5)

parameters.name: Initialize
parameters.desc: Initialization method: DCMode (use DC solution as initial guess)

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver: ILS or Direct

parameters.name: GMRES
parameters.desc: GMRES iterative solver options: Tolerance, MaxIterations, Restart

parameters.name: SolveSpectrum
parameters.desc: Reference to a predefined solve spectrum for spectrum truncation (MDFT mode)

parameters.name: Derivative
parameters.desc: Overwrite Derivative flag of Math section for HB Newton process

Plugin.description: Controls a Gummel-type iterative loop over two or more Coupled statements. Used when a fully coupled Newton method would consume too many resources or diverge. The standard Gummel iteration solves each semiconductor equation (Poisson, Electron, Hole) consecutively. Plugin commands can be nested to create complex iterative solve patterns.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of outer Plugin iterations

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of significant digits for convergence check

parameters.name: ErrRef
parameters.desc: Reference error parameters per equation

parameters.name: Error
parameters.desc: Absolute error parameters per equation

parameters.name: RelErrControl
parameters.desc: Use relative error control (default)

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method (e.g., Blocked, ILS)

Vsource_pset.description: Defines a voltage source compact model instance in the System section for mixed-mode circuit simulations. Supports DC, piecewise-linear (PWL), pulse, sine, and exponential waveforms. The voltage source is connected between the positive node and the negative (ground) node.

parameters.name: dc
parameters.desc: DC voltage value [V]

parameters.name: sine
parameters.desc: Sine waveform parameters: (V_offset V_amplitude freq t_delay phase)

parameters.name: pulse
parameters.desc: Pulse waveform parameters: (V1 V2 t_delay t_rise t_fall t_on t_period)

parameters.name: pwl
parameters.desc: Piecewise-linear waveform: (t1 V1 t2 V2 ...)

parameters.name: ac
parameters.desc: AC magnitude for small-signal AC analysis [V]

parameters.name: temp
parameters.desc: Instance operating temperature [C]

CoordinateSystem.description: Specifies the coordinate system used for explicit coordinates in the Sentaurus Device command file. Affects current plot statements, lattice parameters, and other features that use explicit coordinates. If the coordinate system of the device is incompatible, Sentaurus Device automatically transforms the device into the required coordinate system.

parameters.name: AsIs
parameters.desc: (Default) No transformation; device coordinates are compatible with explicit coordinates

parameters.name: DFISE
parameters.desc: In 2D: x across surface, y into device. In 3D: x,y span surface, z points up

parameters.name: UCS
parameters.desc: Unified coordinate system: x into device, y across surface (2D), z right-handed (3D)

SaveOptField.description: Saves optical field data during an LED simulation within a Quasistationary or Transient ramp. Uses the same syntax as the Plot command (Range, Intervals, MinSpacing) to control at which ramping points the optical field data is written. The output file name is specified by SaveOptField in the File section.

parameters.name: Range
parameters.desc: Range of ramping variable (t) for saving optical field data

parameters.name: Intervals
parameters.desc: Number of intervals within the range for saving data

parameters.name: MinSpacing
parameters.desc: Minimum spacing between consecutive save points

Coupled.description: Activates a Newton-like solver over a set of equations such as Poisson, Electron, Hole, Circuit, Contact, and thermal equations. This is the primary solve command in Sentaurus Device. Parameters control Newton iteration count, convergence precision, and linear solver selection. The Coupled statement can also appear as a standalone equation keyword without braces.

parameters.name: Iterations
parameters.desc: Maximum number of Newton iterations

parameters.name: Method
parameters.desc: Linear solver method: Blocked, ILS, ParDiSo, Direct, Super

parameters.name: SubMethod
parameters.desc: Inner method for block-decomposition solvers

parameters.name: Digits
parameters.desc: Number of significant digits for relative error control

parameters.name: RelErrControl
parameters.desc: Use relative error control (default)

parameters.name: IncompleteNewton
parameters.desc: Enable incomplete Newton (Newton-Richardson) algorithm

QuasiStationary.description: Ramps a device from one solution to another by modifying boundary conditions (contact voltages, currents, charges), quasi-Fermi potentials in doping wells, or physical model parameters. Internally ramps a variable t from 0.0 to 1.0, with step control via MaxStep, MinStep, InitialStep, Increment, and Decrement. The inner solve command is executed at each step.

parameters.name: InitialStep
parameters.desc: Size of the first ramping step (default: 0.1)

parameters.name: MaxStep
parameters.desc: Maximum ramping step size (default: 1)

parameters.name: MinStep
parameters.desc: Minimum ramping step size; simulation exits if step falls below this (default: 0.001)

parameters.name: Increment
parameters.desc: Multiplier for step size when the previous step converged (default: 2)

parameters.name: Decrement
parameters.desc: Divisor for step size when the previous step failed (default: 2)

parameters.name: Goal
parameters.desc: Target values for boundary condition ramping, e.g. Goal {Name="drain" Voltage=5}

ParameterVariation.description: Specifies deterministic parameter variations for sensitivity analysis. Each variation modifies a physical model parameter to compute its cumulative impact on device characteristics.

Doping.description: Enables random dopant fluctuation models for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) noise analysis. Specifies spatial correlation properties of doping variations.

Tunneling.description: Activates tunneling-related physics models. Used as a top-level keyword to enable various tunneling mechanisms including Fowler-Nordheim, direct tunneling, and band-to-band tunneling.

Error.description: Sets the precision target (error tolerance) for nonlinear solver convergence. An optional carrier argument (Electron/Hole) allows carrier-specific error control. Default is 1e-5 eV.

Broadening.description: Activates gain-broadening models or nonlinear gain saturation for quantum well simulations. Supported models include Lorentzian, Landsberg, and Hyperbolic-Cosine broadening.

TimeDependence.description: Specifies the type of time dependency for optical generation in transient simulations. Controls how optical excitation varies with time during transient analysis.

MeshDomain.description: Defines a mesh domain for localized mesh refinement or restricted computation regions. Used to confine certain numerical operations to specific subregions of the device.

Radiation.description: Enables radiation-induced carrier generation models including gamma radiation, alpha particles, and heavy ions. Specifies radiation source parameters and geometry.

MultiValley.description: Activates multivalley band structure statistics for accurate modeling of carrier transport in materials with multiple conduction/valence band minima (e.g., Si delta-valleys). Default is off.

hBandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift for the valence band using a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model with an optional constructor argument. Used for apparent band-edge shift calculations.

IntensityDistribution.signature: IntensityDistribution( Gaussian( Sigma=<float> Length=<float> PeakPosition=<float> PeakWidth=<float> Scaling=<float> ) | Rectangle( dx=<float> dy=<float> ) )
IntensityDistribution.description: Specifies the spatial intensity profile within an illumination window. Supports Gaussian and rectangular distributions for optical excitation.

RayDistribution.description: Creates a ray distribution section to be used in conjunction with an excitation illumination window for raytracing. Defines how excitation parameters translate into raytracing context including starting ray positions and directions.

Model.description: Specifies a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for a given species or physical quantity. The model name and the target species are provided as arguments.

SourceRaysFromFile.description: Imports a set of starting ray direction vectors from a file for LED raytracing simulations. The number of imported rays per vertex is specified by RaysPerVertex.

SourcePosition.description: Specifies the position of a radiation or optical source within the device geometry. Used to define the spatial origin of incoming rays or particles.

DevicePower.description: Defines a break criterion based on the total power dissipation in the device. The simulation stops when the absolute value of device power exceeds the specified threshold. Synonym of OuterDevicePower.

GridAdaptation.description: Controls grid adaptation/mesh refinement during simulation. Specifies criteria for automatic mesh refinement based on solution gradients or error indicators.

RecGenHeat.description: Activates generation-recombination heat sources in the energy conservation equations. By default, these terms are excluded; this keyword enables the H_n, H_p, and H_L contributions from optical generation, impact ionization, and recombination.

h.description: Horizontal polarization identifier for optical/raytracing simulations. Used to specify the polarization state of incident light or rays.

v.description: Vertical polarization identifier for optical/raytracing simulations. Used to specify the polarization state of incident light or rays.

MATH.description: Section keyword for specifying mathematical and numerical parameters. Controls solver settings, convergence criteria, discretization options, and other numeric method configurations.

VCSEL.description: Enables vertical-cavity surface-emitting laser (VCSEL) simulation models. Configures optical cavity, mirror reflectivity, and stimulated emission parameters for VCSEL device analysis.

Circle.description: Defines a circular geometry for optical illumination windows or sensor configurations in raytracing simulations. Specifies the radius of the circular region.

Current.description: Specifies a target current value for a contact boundary condition in mixed-mode or quasistationary simulations. The contact voltage is adjusted to achieve the specified current.

RaysRandomOffset.description: Randomizes the angular shift of starting rays in LED raytracing simulations. Without this keyword, a fixed angular shift determined by the active vertex number is used. An optional random seed ensures reproducibility.

FromElementGrid.description: Specifies that data should be read from or interpolated on the element grid rather than the vertex grid. Used for certain numerical operations requiring element-centered quantities.

ClusterActive.description: Activates the clustering option for LED raytracing, which groups active vertices into clusters to reduce the total number of rays emitted. Improves raytracing performance for devices with many emission centers.

ObservationNode.description: Specifies circuit nodes for which noise spectral densities or deviation analysis is computed during AC-coupled simulations. Must be a subset of the nodes listed in the Node parameter of the ACCoupled statement.

ParDiSo.description: Selects the parallel supernodal direct solver (Parallel Direct Solver). Provides high accuracy and parallel performance for small to medium-sized problems. Supports iterative refinement and nonsymmetric permutations.

Traps.description: Defines trap levels and their properties for recombination, trapping noise, degradation, and trap-assisted tunneling models. Specifies trap concentration, energy level, cross sections, and spatial distributions.

TEPower.description: Activates the thermoelectric power model for semiconductor regions. Supports an analytic formula or a user-defined PMI model. Used in conjunction with the thermodynamic model for self-heating simulations.

Mechanics.description: Activates the mechanical stress solver to compute stress and strain distributions in the device. Enables stress-dependent mobility, piezoresistance, and deformation potential models.

Randomize.description: Randomizes trap positions and/or energies. When applied to traps, it enables trap randomization for statistical analysis. An optional integer seed controls the random number generator for reproducibility.

Skip.description: Omits certain computations or checks during simulation. In KMC-MIM context, SkipKMC omits the kinetic Monte Carlo simulation entirely. In raytracing, Print(Skip) reduces the number of printed ray paths.

Sweep.description: Defines a parameter sweep for quasistationary or transient simulations. Sweep-specific break criteria can be specified to control when individual sweep steps terminate.

Deformation.description: Activates the deformation potential model for stress-induced band structure changes. Computes strain-dependent shifts in conduction and valence band edges based on deformation potential theory.

SensorSweep.description: Specifies a sensor sweep configuration for far-field raytracing analysis. Visualizes the variation of ray power collected on a latitude or longitude ring band of a sensor surface.

QWLocal.description: Activates the localized quantum-well quantization model for semiconductor regions. Computes quantum correction factors for carrier densities in quantum well structures using a local approximation.

eBandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift for the conduction band using a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model with an optional constructor argument. Used for apparent band-edge shift calculations in multistate configurations.

MetalWorkfunction.signature: MetalWorkfunction( [ Randomize( AverageGrainSize=<float> GrainProbability=(<P1>,...) GrainWorkfunction=(<wf1>,...) RandomSeed=<int> ... ) ] )
MetalWorkfunction.description: Specifies the workfunction of metal regions. Supports workfunction randomization to model grain-structure-induced variability using average grain size, grain probability distribution, and grain workfunction values.

Delaunizer3d.description: Selects the 3D Delaunay triangulation algorithm for mesh processing. Used to enforce Delaunay constraints on the computational grid, improving the quality of box method coefficients and numerical accuracy.

PrintSourceVertices.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), prints the index and coordinates of all active emission vertices into the specified file. Used for debugging and verifying ray source vertex placement in LED raytracing simulations.

GMRES.description: Generalized Minimal Residual linear solver. Requires Method=ILS in Coupled/HBCoupled. Uses an iterative Krylov subspace method to solve the linear system within Newton iterations. Parameters control the maximum iterations, subspace restart size, and convergence tolerance.

LEDSpectrum.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), specifies the starting and ending energy range (eV) and the number of subdivisions for computing the LED emission spectrum. Each energy bin produces rays with the corresponding photon energy for spectral analysis.

Bidirectional.description: In Optics(OpticalSolver(BPMScalar(Bidirectional()))), activates the bidirectional beam propagation method (BPM). Performs both forward and backward passes iteratively until convergence. Controls the maximum number of forward/backward iterations and the relative error break criterion.

ComplexRefractiveIndex.description: Activates the complex refractive index (CRI) model for optical simulations. Controls carrier, gain, temperature, and wavelength dependencies of the refractive index (n) and extinction coefficient (k). Supports user-defined PMI models via CRIModel.

OpticsFromFile.description: Flag to read optical generation data from a file instead of computing it during simulation. Used in OpticalGeneration{} to load pre-computed optical profiles, avoiding repeated raytracing or BPM calculations.

Meshing.description: Section keyword for mesh refinement and adaptation controls in Sentaurus Device. Specifies parameters for adaptive meshing strategies during simulation, including refinement criteria and maximum element sizes.

eHCSDegradation.description: Electron hot-carrier stress degradation model. Generates interface traps due to hot-carrier damage at semiconductor/insulator interfaces. Used within Physics{Traps()} at a MaterialInterface. Options include SHE distribution, bond dispersion, and energy interval discretization.

ParallelLicence.description: In the Math section, determines the behavior when insufficient parallel (thread) licenses are available. Controls whether the simulation falls back to fewer threads or aborts when license limits are exceeded.

Charge.description: In the Math section, refers to charge-based error control or charge computation options. Used as a qualifier for quantities such as ErrRef(Charge) to set reference values for charge-related convergence criteria in Newton iterations.

AvaHomotopy.description: In the Math section, activates the avalanche homotopy continuation method for improving Newton convergence in breakdown simulations. Gradually introduces avalanche generation terms to help the solver converge at high bias near breakdown conditions.

FEScalar.description: Finite element scalar option in the Math section. Controls scalar finite element discretization settings for specific equation types or solution variables.

TMM.description: Transfer Matrix Method optical solver. Computes optical field distribution in multilayer structures by solving the wave equation in 1D through the layer stack. Options control intensity pattern type, node density per wavelength, propagation mode, rough interface scattering, and layer stack extraction from the device geometry.

Grading.description: In Physics{} for thermoelectric or material property specification, defines graded material composition or properties that vary spatially across the device structure. Used for thermoelectric materials and compositionally graded alloys.

IALMob.description: Inversion and Accumulation Layer Mobility model. Computes mobility degradation at semiconductor-insulator interfaces due to surface roughness and Coulomb scattering. Options include auto-orientation for parameter selection, clustering, full PhuMob expressions, and phonon combination modes.

MetalTEPower.description: Thermoelectric power model for metals. Uses a PMI model to compute the Seebeck coefficient and thermoelectric behavior in metal regions. Specified as Physics(Material="Copper") { MetalTEPower(pmi_model) }.

NewtonPlot.description: Activates convergence monitoring for Newton iterations. Produces plots of equation residuals and damping factors versus iteration count. Automatically activated when MinStep drops below a threshold controlled by AutoNPMinStepFactor.

NonLocal.description: Declares a named nonlocal mesh in the Math section. Nonlocal meshes are used for tunneling and nonlocal recombination models that require connectivity information beyond the standard finite element mesh, such as band-to-band tunneling and trap-assisted tunneling.

Minimum.description: In the Math section, specifies minimum values for various numerical parameters such as grid element sizes (minimum size of the coarsest grid) or convergence thresholds.

ErrRef.description: Reference error value for convergence control in Newton iterations. Sets the scaling factor for relative error computation of a specific quantity (e.g., ErrRef(Electron), ErrRef(Hole), ErrRef(Charge)). The solver converges when the error drops below this reference.

BalMob.description: Ballistic mobility model for carrier transport. Accounts for ballistic transport effects in short-channel devices. Options include Fermi-Dirac correction, Frensley final mobility rule, kinetic energy formulation, explicit channel length, and temperature dependence.

Aniso.description: Activates anisotropic physical models. Supports anisotropic mobility (electrons/holes), avalanche generation, quantum potential (density gradient), metal resistivity, permittivity, and thermal conductivity. The anisotropy direction can be specified explicitly or by crystal/system coordinates.

eSubband.description: Multivalley electron subband mobility model for stress simulations. Computes stress-induced electron mobility changes using subband structure calculations. Options control distribution function, effective mass, scattering models, and stress effects on drift-diffusion and ballistic mobility.

PeriodicBC.description: Activates periodic boundary conditions (PBCs) for the device simulation. The Direction parameter specifies the axis of periodicity: 0 for x-axis, 1 for y-axis, and 2 for z-axis. Used for simulating periodic device structures such as finFET arrays.

p.description: Single-letter qualifier in the Math section, typically used to designate hole-related quantities in error control and convergence criteria (e.g., ErrRef(p) for hole error reference).

OpticalSolver.description: In Physics{Optics(OpticalSolver(...))}, selects the optical solver method. Options include RayTracing for geometric optics, BPMScalar for scalar beam propagation, and TMM for transfer matrix method. Each solver has its own set of configuration parameters.

Set.description: In the Math section, used to assign values to mathematical or numerical parameters. Also used as a plot dataset qualifier in the Plot section for setting visualization options.

t.description: Single-letter qualifier in the Math section, typically used to designate time-related quantities in transient simulation error control and convergence criteria.

Thermal.description: In the Math section, activates or configures thermal simulation options. Controls heat equation coupling, thermoelectric effects, and self-heating numerical settings.

Angular.description: In Optics sections, specifies angular discretization parameters for optical solvers. For example, in TMM(Scattering(Angular Discretization=<int>)), sets the angular discretization of the interval used in the scattering solver.

Tone.description: In Solve{HBCoupled(Tone(...))} and Math{HB{}}, defines a tone for harmonic balance analysis. Specifies the base frequency and the number of harmonics for multi-tone (MDFT) simulations. Multiple Tone statements define multi-tone excitation.

Gamma.description: In eQuantumPotential() and hQuantumPotential(), specifies the gamma factor for the density gradient quantization model. Gamma=EffectiveMass sets the quantization mass to the density-of-states mass. A PMI model can be used for spatially varying gamma.

MVMLDAcontrols.description: Controls for the modified local-density approximation (MLDA) model. Specifies parameters such as the minimum distance to contacts where the MLDA quantum correction is switched off. Default minimum distance is 75e-8 um.

GaussianQuadrature.description: In Math{BroadeningIntegration(GaussianQuadrature(Order))}, uses Gaussian quadrature to integrate the broadening spectrum in LED simulations. Specifies the quadrature order for numerical integration of gain/spontaneous emission spectra.

ThinLayer.description: Thin-layer mobility model. Computes mobility degradation in thin semiconductor layers (e.g., ultrathin body SOI, FinFETs) by extracting the local layer thickness and applying surface scattering models. Can be combined with IALMob or Lombardi for interface scattering.

PrintORArays.description: Outputs optical ray data for visualization and analysis. Prints ray information from optical simulations (raytracing) to the output file for post-processing or debugging purposes.

eMultiValley.description: Activates the multivalley electron transport model. Computes mobility using multiple conduction band valleys with valley-dependent populations and effective masses. The MLDA option combines with modified local-density approximation for quantum correction. Used for stress-dependent mobility simulations.

DiffusionNoise.description: Diffusion noise model in Physics{Noise(DiffusionNoise(...))}. Computes small-signal noise spectral density due to carrier diffusion processes. The eTemperature and hTemperature options use carrier temperatures instead of lattice temperature in the noise computation.

Rectangle.description: In the Math section, defines a rectangular geometric region for localized operations such as spatial constraints, mesh refinement zones, or domain restrictions.

RHSCoef.description: In Physics{HydrogenReaction()}, defines the right-hand side stoichiometric coefficients that specify the number of particles created in a hydrogen transport reaction. Each species coefficient defaults to 0.

MSConfigs.description: Manipulates multistate configurations (MSCs) during a simulation, used inside Set() in the Solve section. Allows freezing MSCs or setting specific MSC state occupations by device name and state name.

MLDAbox.signature: MLDAbox (MinX=<float> MaxX=<float> MinY=<float> MaxY=<float> MinZ=<float> MaxZ=<float>)
MLDAbox.description: Defines a box region for the modified local-density approximation (MLDA) model. Specifies the spatial bounds (in micrometers) within which the MLDA quantum correction is applied for interface scattering calculations.

SponEmissionIntegration.description: Specifies numeric integration method for spontaneous emission calculations in LED and gain simulations. When used with GaussianQuadrature, replaces default integration with Gaussian quadrature of the specified order, accelerating gain computations.

TransientError.description: Specifies the transient error tolerance for local truncation error (LTE) based time-step control. Activated by CheckTransientError in Math or Transient. Controls time-step adaptation by monitoring charge update error between successive steps.

GateCurrent.description: Configures gate current models at material interfaces (e.g., Silicon/Oxide). Supports Fowler-Nordheim tunneling, direct tunneling (Schenk), lucky electron model (eLucky/hLucky), Fiegna model, SHE distribution model, and PMI-based hot-carrier injection models.

Criterion.description: Convergence criterion keyword used in the Math section for controlling Newton iteration convergence. The Newton iterations stop when both the norm of the right-hand side and the relative error of the solution variables fall below specified tolerances (Digits).

RecBoxIntegr.description: Enables integration of beam distribution over a rectangular window for optical generation computation in the transfer matrix method (TMM). Controls how the optical generation profile is computed from the beam intensity distribution across the illumination window.

Fermi.description: Activates Fermi-Dirac statistics instead of default Boltzmann statistics for carrier concentration computations. Used as an option within Mobility models (e.g., BalMob, eSubband, hSubband, hSixband) to account for carrier degeneracy effects on mobility at high doping or high carrier concentrations.

Laser.description: Configures laser diode simulation physics, including optical cavity effects, stimulated emission, and gain clamping above threshold. Contains similar electrothermal and quantum-well physics as LED simulations but adds threshold-dependent carrier behavior and optical feedback.

Transition.description: Defines a transition between two states of a multistate configuration (MSC). Specifies the interacting state (From), the reference state (To), optional capture/emission PMI model (CEModel), particle conservation reservoirs, and field computation options.

TraceSource.description: Activates ray source tracing in LED raytracing simulations. Correlates output rays to their originating active vertices by accumulating ray intensity at each active vertex into an LED_TraceSource profile, identifying the best regions for current channeling to improve extraction efficiency.

HydrogenReaction.description: Defines chemical reactions between mobile hydrogen species (atoms, ions, molecules, species A/B/C) and charge carriers (electrons, holes) in hydrogen transport simulations. Specifies forward/reverse reaction coefficients, activation energies, field coefficients, and particle conservation.

Polygon.description: Defines a polygon-shaped illumination window in 3D optical simulations. Specified as a series of vertices in the local 2D coordinate system of the window. Used within Window() in the Physics{Optics{Excitation}} section to define the spatial extent of incident optical excitation.

eNMP.description: Extended nonradiative multiphonon (eNMP) model for trap-assisted tunneling and degradation. Specifies precursor concentration, spatial distribution via SFactor (PMI model or dataset), and number of random samples for statistical analysis.

freq.description: Specifies the frequency parameter for harmonic balance (HB) and AC analysis. Defines the fundamental excitation frequency for periodic or quasi-periodic simulations, used in HBCoupled statements and circuit source definitions (e.g., sine = (vo va freq ...)).

BPM.description: Beam propagation method for optical simulation of waveguide structures. Solves the paraxial wave equation along the propagation direction to compute the optical field distribution. Supports bidirectional propagation, PML boundary conditions, Gaussian and plane-wave excitation, and configurable reference refractive index.

Extrapolate.description: Controls solution extrapolation for accelerating Quasistationary and Transient simulations. Uses previous solution as initial guess for the next step. Configurable extrapolation order, algorithm selection, minimum step size, and density limit below which extrapolation is skipped.

Time.description: Specifies the time parameter for transient analysis or radiation generation timing. In transient simulations, sets the simulation time point. In radiation models (AlphaParticle, HeavyIon), defines the time at which charge generation peaks.

UserWindow.description: Defines a user-defined window of rays for optical raytracing simulations. Allows importing custom ray directions from a file, setting the number of rays, and configuring polarization vectors. Used within Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver{Raytracing(...)}}} or Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver{FDTD{...}}}}.

AxisAligned2d.description: Specifies axis-aligned geometry constraint for 2D mesh operations. Used in mesh generation and geometry processing to align mesh elements with the coordinate axes, simplifying mesh structure for planar device geometries.

AxisAligned3d.description: Specifies axis-aligned geometry constraint for 3D mesh operations. Used in mesh generation and geometry processing to align mesh elements with the coordinate axes, simplifying mesh structure for rectangular device geometries.

NonLocalPath.description: Controls handling of nonlocal path derivatives in the Math section, applicable to Quasistationary and Transient statements. Configures derivative computation, frozen tunneling direction, step-size control boundaries, Newton iteration count for Jacobian collection, and postprocessing mode.

SimStats.description: Activates writing of simulation statistics to the current plot file (.plt) after each successful solution. Outputs a SimStats group of datasets including iteration counts, convergence residuals, time-step information, and other solver diagnostics for visualization.

HeatSource.description: Specifies a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for custom heat source definitions in thermodynamic simulations. The PMI model computes the local heat generation rate, which is added to the lattice heat equation. Used in the Physics section of the command file.

hQuantumPotential.description: Density gradient quantum correction model for holes. Computes quantum-confined potential shift for holes near semiconductor-insulator interfaces using the density gradient formalism. Supports anisotropic directions, boundary conditions, Gamma factor specification (EffectiveMass or PMI), local model coupling, and named parameter sets.

Strain.description: Activates strain-dependent physics models. In LED simulations, enables strain modeling for quantum wells with a given reference lattice constant. In the Model section, enables deformation potential, piezoresistive mobility, and multivalley band structure models for stress/strain simulations.

hMultiValley.description: Activates the multivalley band structure model for holes only. Accounts for multiple valence band valleys in band structure computations, affecting density of states, carrier concentrations, and strain-induced band edge shifts. The DensityIntegral option enables numeric Gauss-Laguerre integration for Fermi-Dirac integrals.

RayTracing.description: Unified raytracing interface for optical generation computation in the Physics{Optics{OpticalSolver}} section. Configures ray propagation parameters including depth limits, minimum intensity thresholds, far-field sensors, Monte Carlo mode, external material CRI, polarization, user-defined ray windows, and interface flux plotting.

DensityIntegral.description: Activates numeric Gauss-Laguerre integration for the Fermi-Dirac integral F_1/2 in multivalley band structure calculations. Used as an option within (e|h)Multivalley for testing and comparison with the analytic approximation. Specified in the Physics section.

Barrier.description: Configures barrier-related physics models at interfaces. Used in eBarrierTunneling/hBarrierTunneling for nonlocal tunneling with options like BarrierLowering, Schroedinger-based tunneling probabilities, TwoBand dispersion, and Transmission coefficients. Also used in SingletExciton for specifying barrier type (CondBand/ValBand) at heterointerfaces.

Mobility.description: Configures carrier mobility models including doping dependence (Arora, Masetti, PhuMob), high-field saturation (CaugheyThomas, TransferredElectronEffect), interface degradation (Lombardi, IALMob, Enormal), and strain-dependent models (eSubband, hSixband, piezoresistive factor/tensor). Applied per carrier type with eMobility/hMobility variants.

DeterministicProblem.description: Specifies a deterministic (non-statistical) variation problem in the IFM (Impedance Field Method) section. Used for systematic parameter sweeps such as deterministic doping variations, geometric variations, or parameter variations, as opposed to randomized statistical variations.

Boundary.description: Configures boundary conditions in various physics contexts. In BPM, specifies PML (perfectly matched layer) absorbing boundary conditions with grid nodes, stretching parameters, and order. In optics, sets reflection/transmission properties. In raytracing, defines special contact boundary conditions for ray behavior.

eQuantumPotential.description: Density gradient quantum correction model for electrons. Computes quantum-confined potential shift for electrons near semiconductor-insulator interfaces using the density gradient formalism. Supports anisotropic directions, boundary conditions, Gamma factor specification (EffectiveMass or PMI), local model coupling, and named parameter sets.

Integrate.description: Computes the spatial integral of a specified quantity over a domain in the CurrentPlot section. The domain can be a region, material, semiconductor, or everywhere. Outputs the integrated value to the current plot file. Also used in Math section for tracking additional data.

LED.description: Configures light-emitting diode (LED) simulation physics including quantum-well band structure (zincblende/wurtzite), radiative and stimulated emission coefficients (PMI or built-in), gain broadening models, spontaneous emission integration, QW transport, and raytracing optics. Supports strain effects and spontaneous emission scaling.

DFB.description: Configures distributed feedback (DFB) laser simulation physics. DFB lasers use a built-in grating structure for optical feedback, requiring coupled-wave analysis of the optical field. Used in conjunction with laser/LED physics models for simulating spectral properties and threshold behavior of DFB laser diodes.

Degradation.description: Hot-carrier stress degradation model for MOS-based devices. Includes trap degradation kinetics, reaction enhancement factors, hydrogen diffusion in oxide, and activated barrier double well thermionic model for NBTI/PBTI. Invoked within Traps specification to model interface trap generation and threshold voltage shift over device lifetime.

SingletExciton.description: Activates the singlet exciton equation for OLED/organic semiconductor simulations. Configured in the Coupled statement of the Solve section and regionwise in Physics. Supports bimolecular generation, radiative and trap-assisted radiative decay, nonradiative quenching (eQuenching/hQuenching), and interface dissociation.

IncompleteNewton.description: Enables the incomplete Newton method where the Jacobian matrix is reused across iterations instead of being recomputed each time. The Jacobian is reused if both RhsFactor and UpdateFactor conditions are satisfied. Can be specified globally in Math or locally as an option in Coupled statements.

HBPlot.description: Specifies quantities to plot from harmonic balance (HB) simulations. Controls output of circuit currents and voltages in both frequency domain (HB_F) and time domain (HB_T). Supports filtering by harmonic order and dataset names. Used in the Plot section and System section for circuit quantities.

LatticeTemperature.description: Configures lattice temperature physics for nonisothermal simulations. Activates thermodynamic equations including heat capacity, thermal conductivity, and Joule heat models. Used in BreakCriteria to set maximum temperature limits. Also used as a PMI data field (LatticeTemperature) for postprocessing.

Absorption.description: In Physics{Optics{}}, activates optical absorption computation in nonsemiconductor regions or materials. When specified inside RayTracing(), enables absorption calculation for optical generation in insulator regions.

Add2TotalDoping.description: In Traps(), includes the trap concentration in the acceptor, donor, and total doping concentrations used to compute mobility, lifetimes, and other quantities. With ChargedTraps option, only includes charged trap concentrations.

Affinity.description: Uses a PMI (Physical Model Interface) model for electron affinity. Allows user-defined or parameter-file-based specification of the electron affinity for a region or material.

Average.description: In the Math section, activates averaging algorithms for anisotropic model computations. Related options include AverageAniso (average-aniso approximation) and AverageBoxMethod (element-oriented box method for integration).

BandEdgeShift.description: Computes band-edge shift due to various physical effects. The electron variant eBandEdgeShift and hole variant hBandEdgeShift use PMI models to compute conduction and valence band-edge shifts respectively.

BandgapNarrowing.description: Selects the bandgap narrowing (BGN) model used in EffectiveIntrinsicDensity. Supported models include Slotboom, OldSlotboom, BennettWilson (default), delAlamo, JainRoulston, and TableBGN. Use NoBandGapNarrowing to disable.

BarrierType.description: In SingletExciton() at heterointerfaces, selects the barrier type for singlet exciton flux injection. Options: Bandgap (default, uses bandgap difference), CondBand (conduction band-edge difference), ValBand (valence band-edge difference).

BroadeningIntegration.description: In the Math section, specifies the numeric integration method for gain broadening calculations in LED/laser simulations. Uses Gaussian quadrature to accelerate gain table computations including broadening effects.

Condition.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a Boolean Tcl expression as a break condition for stopping the simulation. In Select() for spectrum files, defines a Boolean expression to select a subset of a multidimensional spectrum.

CurrentDensity.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a break criterion based on current density (A/cm^2). The simulation stops when the current density exceeds the specified maximum value. DevName identifies the device in mixed-mode simulations.

Dipole.description: At insulator-insulator interfaces, activates the dipole layer model for immobile charges causing a potential jump across the interface. Reference specifies the reference side of the interface (region or material name). Parameters include sigma_D and epsilon.

DopingVariation.description: In RandomizedVariation(), activates doping profile variations for statistical impedance field method (sIFM) simulations. Generates random spatial variations of the doping concentration.

DualGridInterpolation.description: In the Math section, controls interpolation between dual grids (for example, between the optical grid and the device mesh). Method=Simple uses simple interpolation algorithm for mapping data between different grid systems.

EffectiveIndex.description: In optics and waveguide simulations, computes the effective refractive index of optical modes. Used in BPM (Beam Propagation Method) and VCSEL simulations for modal analysis.

EffectiveIntrinsicDensity.description: Selects the effective intrinsic carrier density model. Controls how bandgap narrowing affects the intrinsic carrier concentration ni. Options include BandGapNarrowing(Slotboom), BandGapNarrowing(BennettWilson), NoBandGapNarrowing, or a PMI model name.

EnormalInterface.description: In Physics{} at interfaces, activates the electric field normal to the interface for physics model evaluation. Used for interface mobility degradation and other interface-dependent models where the normal electric field component is needed.

eSHEDistribution.description: In Traps(), specifies the six parameters of the electron energy-dependent terms of the depassivation constant for the trap degradation model: delta_SHE, epsilon_th, epsilon_a, delta_perp, rho_perp, and rho_SHE. Used for HCS degradation modeling.

Excitation.description: In Physics{Optics{}}, defines the optical excitation configuration including illumination windows (circle, rectangle, polygon, line) and their spatial intensity distributions. Specifies the optical source geometry for raytracing and TMM solvers.

ExternalBoltzmannSolver.description: Coupling interface to the external Subband-BTE solver of Sentaurus Device QTX. Enables quantum corrections and effective mobility extraction from the subband Boltzmann transport equation solver. Configures slices, volume restriction, damping length, and mobility options.

FEVectorial.description: In the Math section, selects the vectorial finite element formulation for solving the electromagnetic wave equation. Used in optical simulations (BPM, FDTD) where the full vector nature of the electromagnetic field must be resolved.

Gaussian.description: In HeavyIon(), uses a Gaussian spatial distribution for the charge generation track. In Traps(), specifies a Gaussian energetic distribution for trap energy levels. In SpatialShape options, uses a Gaussian shape function for spatial restriction.

hBarrierTunneling.description: In Traps(), activates hole barrier tunneling (trap-assisted tunneling from the valence band). NonLocal option enables tunneling from named nonlocal meshes. TwoBand option uses two-band dispersion for the WKB tunneling probability.

HydrogenMolecule.description: Hydrogen molecule species (H2) for hydrogen diffusion simulations. Used in Physics{HydrogenDiffusion()} to configure transport of molecular hydrogen. Must be coupled in the Solve section via Coupled{...HydrogenMolecule}. Plotted as HydrogenMolecule in the Plot section.

i.description: Single-letter qualifier designating the imaginary part of a complex quantity in AC or harmonic balance analysis. Used in System{} and Plot{} sections to reference the imaginary component of voltages, currents, or other complex variables.

ImportDomain.description: In OpticalGeneration{ReadFromFile()}, controls interpolation when loading optical generation profiles. Specifies source and destination regions, domain truncation boxes, and grid mapping between source and destination grids.

InnerDevicePower.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a break criterion based on inner device power dissipation (W/um^d). The simulation stops when the inner device power exceeds the specified limits. Syntax mirrors DevicePower but computes only the inner power.

Line.description: In Window() (Excitation section), defines a line-shaped illumination window for optical excitation. In Sensor() (far field), defines a line sensor with two corner points. In ComputeCarrierPath(), defines an end condition for carrier path tracing.

Node.description: In mixed-mode circuit simulations, references a circuit node by name. Used in BreakCriteria{Current(Node=<ident>)} to define break conditions on specific circuit nodes, and in System{} for node voltage/current specifications.

Noise.description: Activates noise analysis using the impedance field method (IFM). Specifies local noise source (LNS) models such as DiffusionNoise, GenerationRecombination, TrapNoise, and others. The optional string name allows multiple noise specifications in one simulation.

PostProcess.description: In the Math section, specifies a PMI postprocessing routine to be called after each transient time step. The string identifies the PMI postprocess model name.

PrintRayInfo.description: In LED(Optics(RayTrace())), prints all indices and positions of starting rays into a specified file. Used for debugging LED raytracing simulations by recording the origin of each ray.

Schroedinger.description: Solves the 1D Schrodinger equation for quantum-confined carriers along a nonlocal line. Activated with Active(Type=QuantumWell). Options include Electron/Hole (carrier types), DensityTail, EnergyInterval, MaxSolutions, Smooth, and polarization settings for optical matrix elements.

Stripe.description: In optical simulations, defines a stripe-shaped excitation geometry or gain region. Typically used in edge-emitting laser and waveguide simulations to define the lateral extent of the optical mode or pumped region.

StimEmissionCoeff.description: In LED(), specifies a PMI model for the stimulated emission coefficient. Used to import gain and spontaneous emission data via PMI for accurate LED and laser simulations.

time.description: In the Plot section, specifies the time variable as a dataset for plotting. The time dataset is automatically available in transient simulations and can be plotted alongside other quantities in Sentaurus Visual.

TransientErrRef.description: In the Math section, sets the reference value for absolute error control in transient time-step adaptation. When used with Charge, the reference value is the elementary charge (1.602e-19 C). Controls the local truncation error for transient step acceptance.

Unset.description: In Solve{}, unsets circuit nodes, disconnecting them from device electrodes. With TrapFilling option, reverts to standard trap equations (disables explicit trap occupation). Used in mixed-mode simulations for dynamic circuit topology changes.

Voltage.signature: Voltage(absval=<float>, maxval=<float>, minval=<float>, contact=<string>, Node=<ident>, DevName=<ident>)
Voltage.description: In BreakCriteria{}, defines a voltage break criterion. The simulation stops when the voltage at a contact or circuit node exceeds the specified limits. Uses the same syntax as Current for specifying contact, node, and device identifiers.

Volume.description: Specifies a named volume for physics model restriction. Used in BandEdge() within RandomizedVariation() to confine band-edge variations, in Conductivity() for metal conductivity variations, and in ExternalBoltzmannSolver() to restrict quantum corrections to a specific domain.

When.description: In Solve{}, defines conditional execution of solve commands. The enclosed statements are executed only when the associated condition (typically defined in BreakCriteria) is met. Used for adaptive simulation strategies and mode switching.

Piezo.description: Configures piezoresistive and stress-dependent models in the Physics section. Specifies stress or strain tensor components, selects mobility tensor models (e.g., Tensor("pmi_model")), and enables stress-dependent PMI models. Constant stress/strain tensors can be specified directly, or read from a TDR file via the File section.

LayerThickness.description: Extracts the quantization length (layer thickness) for quantum correction models such as thin-layer mobility and MLDA. Automatically detects layer boundaries in defined regions. The extracted thickness is used by PMI models (e.g., ReadLayerThickness()) and affects mobility, thermal conductivity, and other layer-dependent properties.

ActiveVertex.description: Used inside Print() in the RayTrace section to restrict ray path output to rays emitted from a specific active vertex only. The integer argument specifies the global index of the active vertex.

ACPlot.description: Specifies which quantities to plot in the AC analysis plot file within the System section. If specified, the given quantities (solution variables, current densities) are plotted alongside AC response data for each AC node voltage excitation and frequency. Without this statement, only node voltages are plotted.

MSConfig.description: Defines a multistate configuration (MSC) within the MSConfigs section of Physics. Describes the states (at least two), transitions between them, and optional band-edge shift models. MSCs model phenomena such as NBTI trap states with charge and hydrogen composition. Used for MSC-dependent PMI models in mobility, recombination, and other physical models.

Medium.description: Specifies the properties of surrounding media (top or bottom) for the extracted layer structure in the LayerStackExtraction section of the TMM optical solver.
