Overrides the default vacuum assumption by specifying a material name or direct refractive index and extinction coefficient values.

Reservoirs.description: Specifies particle reservoirs for MSC transitions that involve charge state changes. The conduction band (CB) serves as an electron reservoir and the valence band (VB) as a hole reservoir. Each reservoir specifies the number of particles exchanged during the transition. Ensures total charge conservation during MSC state transitions.

DebugLEDRadiation.description: Debugging feature for LED raytracing within the RayTrace section. Traces output rays within a specified angular range (in degrees) and above a minimum intensity threshold back to their source active vertices. Results are written to the specified file. In 2D, the angle uses regular polar coordinates; in 3D, the angle is measured from the z-axis (spherical theta).

EnergyRelaxationTime.description: Parameter file section specifying energy-dependent energy relaxation times (tau_w_ele for electrons, tau_w_hol for holes) in picoseconds. Supports constant values, piecewise polynomial approximation (Formula=2), and spline interpolation (Formula=3). Used in thermodynamic transport models to define carrier energy relaxation dynamics.

Piezolectric_Polarization.description: Activates piezoelectric polarization modeling by adding the divergence of the piezoelectric polarization vector as an additional charge term to the Poisson equation. The polarization vector is defined by a PMI model. Relevant for wurtzite crystals (e.g., GaN-based devices) where piezoelectric effects significantly impact carrier distributions. The resulting polarization vector and charge can be plotted as PE_Polarization/vector and PE_Charge.

Split.description: Used inside IncompleteIonization to distribute a single doping species across multiple lattice sites with specified occupation probabilities (weights). The sum of weights must equal one. Sentaurus Device automatically generates the necessary split doping species (e.g., NitrogenConcentration_split1, _split2, etc.) without requiring entries in datexcodes.txt.

ElementLimit.description: Sets the maximum number of finite elements for mesh generation or refinement operations in sdevice simulations. Used in the Math section to control mesh density and simulation resource limits.

Delaunizer2d.description: Selects the 2D Delaunay triangulation algorithm for mesh generation or refinement in sdevice. Used as a math option for controlling the meshing method applied to 2D device structures.

Bandstructure.description: Specifies the crystal structure type for band structure calculations in LED simulations. For example, CrystalType=Wurtzite activates the simplified band structure model for wurtzite crystals, computing band-edge energies and effective masses for heavy hole, light hole, and crystal-field split-off hole bands. Used within the LED section.

hHCSDegradation.description: Hole version of the hot-carrier stress degradation (HCS) model for MOS-based devices. Includes single-particle, multiple-particle, and field-enhanced thermal degradation mechanisms that contribute to bond breakage and interface trap formation. Specified as part of a Traps definition at the material interface. The complementary electron version is eHCSDegradation.

PMIModel.description: Specifies a Plug-In Module (PMI) model by name, with optional index and string arguments passed to the model. Used in various Physics contexts including high-field saturation (e.g., HighFieldSaturation, eHighFieldSaturation, hHighFieldSaturation), doping-dependent mobility, thermal conductivity, heat capacity, nonlocal band-to-band tunneling, and MSC-dependent models. Enables user-defined or MSC-dependent physical models.

DopingWells.signature: DopingWells([Region=<string> | Material=<string> | Semiconductor] [(x1 y1 z1) (x2 y2 z2)])
DopingWells.description: Multiple-well ramping feature in the Solve section. Allows simultaneous ramping of quasi-Fermi potentials for all doping wells within a specified region, material, or all semiconductor wells. Used inside Set() and Goal() to set or ramp electron/hole quasi-Fermi levels across multiple quantum wells during quasistationary simulation.

ExcludeHorizontalSource.description: Within the RayTrace section, excludes source rays emitting within a specified angular range (in degrees) from the horizontal plane (the plane of quantum wells). The power from excluded rays is redistributed equally among remaining rays, producing an approximate anisotropic emission pattern. Used to mitigate unrealistic spikes in LED radiation patterns caused by rays traveling along the quantum well plane.

OutputLightToolsRays.description: Outputs LED starting rays from active vertices to a Synopsys LightTools-compatible ray data file. Used inside RayTrace(Disable(...)) to skip internal raytracing. Supports ASCII and binary formats, spectrum discretization, and ray clustering. The ray file base name is derived from the plot file name with _lighttools.txt or _lighttools.ray extension.

ILS.description: Iterative Linear Solver used for solving linear systems in device simulation. The default solver for 3D structures (with set(1)), while SUPER is default for 1D/2D. Supports preconditioning, ordering, and iterative options configurable via ILSrc in the Math section. Supports MultipleRHS for AC analysis. May require extended precision or specialized preconditioners for MPBC simulations.

OutputT2TPaths.description: Specifies a debug output file for trap-to-trap (T2T) tunneling path information in discrete trap models. Used within DiscreteTrapT2T trap entries to write debugging data about trap-to-trap transition paths. Only needs to be defined once across all trap entries.

HydrogenAtom.description: Species keyword for hydrogen atom transport in the Solve section's Coupled statement. Activates the drift-diffusion equation for hydrogen atoms. Parameters (diffusivity, activation energy, etc.) are set in the HydrogenDiffusion section of the parameter file. The hydrogen atom density can be plotted and its concentration set via Set(). Supports PMI for diffusivity and alpha parameters.

ElectricField.description: Used as an option to Avalanche in the Recombination section to select the electric-field-dependent impact ionization model. Also available as a plot dataset (ElectricField/Vector for vector field visualization) and as an option to HighFieldSaturation for PMI driving force selection (EparallelToInterface vs ElectricField).

FromFile.description: Optical solver that loads absorbed photon density or optical generation profiles from file. More flexible than OpticalGeneration(ReadFromFile), supporting illumination spectra and parameter ramping. Requires ComputeFromMonochromaticSource or ComputeFromSpectrum in the OpticalGeneration section. Supports DatasetName selection, spectral interpolation, and user-defined parameters. Works with TMM, OptBeam, and WeightedAPDintegration features.

Exclude.description: Used in KMC (Kinetic Monte Carlo) defect definitions to specify a list of excluded particle types. Applied within Particle and Filament definitions in the KMCDefects section to prevent certain particles from participating in filament formation or particle interactions.

RandomField.description: Declares a random field for spatially correlated statistical variations within the RandomizedVariation section of the Math section. Specifies correlation function type (Grain, Exponential, Gaussian), correlation length (Lambda or AverageGrainSize), amplitude, and optional volume restriction. When declared, all variations in the same RandomizedVariation section become correlated.

StepFunction.description: Defines a step function cutoff for interband optical absorption in quantum yield or optical absorption heat models. Options: Bandgap uses Eg-Ebgn as cutoff, EffectiveBandgap uses Eg-Ebgn+2*(3/2)kT, Wavelength/Energy specifies an explicit cutoff value. Takes precedence over EffectiveAbsorption if both are specified in QuantumYield.

Sensor.description: Defines a sensor detector for collecting ray power in the Farfield section of the unified raytracing interface. Supports Rectangle (3D), Line (2D), and Angular sensor types. UseNormalFlux sums normal-projected power from impinging rays. AxisAligned (3D only) requires only two opposing corners. Multiple sensors with unique names can be defined.

RefractiveIndex.description: Specifies the real part of the refractive index for a medium surrounding the extracted layer structure in the TMM LayerStackExtraction section. Used as an alternative to specifying a Material name in the Medium section. The corresponding imaginary part (ExtinctionCoefficient) can also be specified.

RectangularWindow.description: Defines a rectangular window of starting rays for raytracing. Specifies three vertices defining the rectangle (two required for 2D, three for 3D), the number of rays along each side, and the ray direction vector. Alternative to UserWindow; only one can be used per raytracing setup.

Raytrace.description: Alternative spelling for RayTrace. Configures the raytracing optical solver within the Optics section. Controls ray emission, clustering, polarization, intensity thresholds, Monte Carlo mode, and output options for LED and optical device simulations.

Maximum.description: Specifies maximum value limits for numerical parameters in the Math section, such as maximum electric field, maximum carrier concentration, or maximum iteration counts. Used to constrain solution variables during Newton iterations to improve convergence.

State.description: Defines a single state within an MSConfig (multistate configuration) in the Physics section. Each MSC requires at least two states. Specifies the state name, charge, hydrogen composition, and optional TtOM (trap-to-oscillator model) flag. States participate in transitions defined by the Transition keyword.

hSHEDistribution.description: Specifies vertex positions at which to plot the hole energy distribution from the spherical harmonics expansion (SHE) method. The distribution is output to the .plt file.

Print.description: Controls printing of simulation information to the log file or current plot. Used in various contexts including raytracing (create raytree in output .tdr file) and general diagnostics.

CEModel.description: Specifies a physical model interface (PMI) model for computing multistate configuration transition rates. Used within Transition statements in MSConfig sections for degradation simulations.

EmissionType.description: Specifies the type of emission model used in radiative recombination calculations for optoelectronic device simulations.

TMM1D.description: Enables the one-dimensional transfer matrix method (TMM) for computing optical generation rates. Used as a boundary condition option for raytracing at interfaces.

FarField.signature: Farfield( Origin | Discretization | ObservationRadius | Sensor | SensorSweep )
FarField.description: Activates the far-field and sensor feature for raytracing optical simulations. Controls the observation hemisphere discretization and angular sensor definitions for light extraction analysis.

DOS.description: Density of states. Used in quantum-confined mobility models (eSubband/hSubband) and SHE method to specify how the density of states is computed, affecting scattering rates and transport properties.

BarrierTunneling.description: Specifies parameters for nonlocal tunneling at interfaces, contacts, and junctions. Defines tunneling effective mass (mt) and other fit parameters. Can be applied per material or to named nonlocal meshes.

Box.signature: Box { MinX MaxX MinY MaxY MinZ MaxZ }
Box.description: Defines a rectangular box region used for spatial restrictions in various math and physics settings, such as limiting quantum potential corrections or density gradient models to specific areas.

EquilibriumSolution.description: Controls the initial equilibrium solution computation before bias is applied. Specifies Newton iteration parameters, error control, and line search damping for the equilibrium solve step.

Recombination.description: Selects recombination models for carrier generation-recombination processes. Supports SRH, Auger, radiative, band-to-band tunneling, and singlet exciton recombination models.

Scattering.description: Specifies the scattering rate model used in multivalley mobility calculations (eSubband/hSubband). MLDA uses modified local density approximation DOS; without argument uses simplified bulk scattering rate.

SetConstant.description: Defines a constant optical generation rate value (in s^-1 cm^-3). Used within OpticalGeneration to specify a spatially uniform, time-independent generation profile.

DeterministicVariation.description: Specifies deterministic variations for sensitivity analysis. Includes doping variations, geometric variations (surface displacement), and parameter variations to compute cumulative impact on device characteristics.

LayerStackExtraction.description: Controls how the layer stack is extracted for 1D optical solvers (TMM or OptBeam). Can operate in RegionWise or ElementWise mode with specified starting position and illumination window reference.

OpticalGeneration.description: Specifies the type of optical generation computation and controls interpolation when loading optical generation profiles. Supports monochromatic source, spectrum, constant value, and file-based approaches with time dependence.

TurningPoints.description: Specifies time-step control parameters for optical generation in transient simulations. Defines limiting time steps for rise, plateau, and fall phases of time-dependent optical signals.

OptBeam.description: Uses the optical beam absorption method to compute optical generation rates. Supports layer stack extraction with region-wise or element-wise mode and illumination window positioning.

HydrogenIon.description: Specifies hydrogen ion (H+) transport properties in the hydrogen diffusion model. Supports PMI models for the thermal diffusion prefactor (alpha) and Arrhenius diffusivity parameters.

eBarrierTunneling.description: Activates nonlocal tunneling for electrons at interfaces and contacts. Options include Band2Band tunneling (Simple/Full/UpsideDown), TwoBand dispersion, Schroedinger-based tunneling probability, Peltier heat, and interface transmission coefficients.

hSubband.signature: hSubband( Fermi | Doping | EffectiveMass | Scattering | BalMob )
hSubband.description: Activates the multivalley hole mobility model with subband quantization effects. Options include Fermi statistics, stress-dependent effective mass, MLDA scattering rate, and ballistic mobility correction.

ConstantCarrierGeneration.description: Defines a constant carrier generation rate for debugging or testing purposes. The value is specified in cm^-3 s^-1. Can be used as a Goal model parameter in quasistationary ramps.

RandomizedVariation.description: Specifies parameters for the statistical impedance field method (sIFM). Controls random field generation with spatial correlations, number of samples, and random seed for Monte Carlo-style variability analysis.

GeometricVariation.description: Specifies deterministic geometric (surface position) variations for sensitivity analysis. Defines amplitude and direction of surface displacement with interpolation options for dielectric and quantum potential terms.

WidthExtraction.description: Controls quantum well width extraction parameters for gain calculations and LED simulations. Specifies chord weight, minimum angles, and the materials or regions adjoining the quantum well.

OuterDevicePower.description: Defines a break criterion based on the outer (dissipated) device power. Simulation stops when the absolute value of the outer power exceeds the specified threshold. Synonym of DevicePower.

LEDRadiationPara.description: Specifies radiation parameters for LED simulations, controlling the optical emission characteristics including spontaneous and stimulated emission properties within the LED physics model.

TrapConcentration.description: Plot quantity showing the separated full bulk trap concentration defined in each trap entry at each trap energy level. Available only in the CurrentPlot statement. TrapConcentrationPerEntry shows the integrated trap concentration per entry.

LayerStructure.description: Defines a multilayer structure for the transfer matrix method (TMM) optical calculation. Each line specifies layer thickness (in um) and material name. Used in RayTraceBC for optical boundary conditions.

Surface.description: Specifies the name of a surface for geometric variation in deterministic sensitivity analysis. The named surface is displaced according to the amplitude parameters defined in the GeometricVariation section.

Optics.description: Specifies the optical solver and related optical physics models. Supports TMM (transfer matrix method), BPMScalar (beam propagation method), and LED-specific raytracing for optical generation computation.

OutputLightToolsFarfieldRays.description: Outputs far-field ray data for interfacing with LightTools optical design software. Controls output file format (ASCII or binary) and wavelength discretization for the exported ray data.

Regexp.description: Regular expression pattern matching for electrode and thermode names. Used in Electrode, Thermode, and other boundary condition sections to match multiple structure contacts with a single specification.

FDTD.description: Specifies the finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) optical solver. Controls tensor mesh generation by Sentaurus Mesh, including options to generate mesh once or at each wavelength change.

ACCompute.description: Controls AC or noise analysis performance within a Quasistationary or Transient ramp. Specifies at which bias points AC analysis is performed, reducing computational cost by avoiding unnecessary AC computations.

ElementSize.description: Controls mesh element size refinement parameters. Used in adaptive meshing and nonlocal mesh construction to specify discretization criteria for numerical accuracy.

LHSCoef.description: Defines particle numbers to be removed (left-hand side coefficients) in hydrogen reaction equations. Specifies how many electrons, holes, and hydrogen species are consumed in each reaction.

MoleFraction.description: Specifies mole fraction values (xMoleFraction, yMoleFraction) for compound semiconductor regions. Supports linear grading from boundaries with configurable grading distances.

Weights.description: Specifies occupation probabilities (weights) for multiple lattice sites when using the Split option in IncompleteIonization. Each weight corresponds to a dopant redistribution fraction across available lattice sites.

ThermalConductivity.description: Specifies the thermal conductivity model for the lattice heat equation. Supports built-in models (ThinLayerKappa), temperature-dependent models, and user-defined PMI models including anisotropic thermal conductivity.

NonlocalPlot.description: Controls the output of nonlocal tunneling path and band-to-band tunneling data for visualization and analysis.

Piezoelectric_Polarization.description: Enables piezoelectric polarization modeling for III-nitride and other polar semiconductor materials (GaN, AlN, InN).

AcceptorConcentration.description: Acceptor doping concentration (NA) at each vertex, specified through the doping profile in the device structure. Used in physics models for computing carrier statistics, mobility, and recombination rates.

Auger.description: Enables Auger recombination model within Recombination(). Auger recombination is a three-carrier process dominant at high carrier concentrations. The optional (WithGeneration) qualifier also includes the Auger generation counterpart.

BE.description: Backward Euler time integration method for transient simulations. A first-order implicit scheme that can be specified either in the Math section (changing the global default) or in the Transient statement options within the Solve section. The default transient method is TRBDF.

Band2Band.description: Band-to-band tunneling model, specified within Recombination() or eBarrierTunneling()/hBarrierTunneling(). Options control the parallel momentum integral treatment: None disables tunneling, Full uses the consistent integral, Simple uses a simplified form, and UpsideDown uses a nonphysical variant.

BandGap.description: Intrinsic band gap energy (Eg) at each vertex, computed by the bandgap model. Available as a plot dataset (unit: eV). Configured through EffectiveIntrinsicDensity() which controls the bandgap and bandgap narrowing models such as Slotboom, OldSlotboom, and Bennett-Wilson.

Blocked.description: Uses the Blocked solver for small-signal AC analysis. The Blocked method employs block decomposition to solve the AC-coupled system, which can be more efficient for certain device configurations.

Circuit.description: Circuit equation solved in mixed-mode simulations. When specified in Load, loads the circuit state from a previous simulation. In the equations table (Table 342), represents the compact circuit equations with a reference value of 0.0258 V and relative error tolerance of 1e-3.

Constant.description: Constant (temperature-independent) lattice heat capacity model within HeatCapacity(). When selected, the heat capacity is treated as a constant value rather than using the default temperature-dependent model.

ConstantMobility.description: Uses constant mobility values within Mobility() if neither PhuMob (Philips unified) nor DopingDependence is specified. Provides a simple constant mobility model as a fallback, typically used for initial debugging or when doping-dependent effects are negligible.

DisplacementCurrent.description: Displacement current density (JD) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset (unit: A/cm^2). Represents the time-varying electric field contribution to total current, significant in transient and AC simulations. Related AC response datasets include ImDisplacementCurrentResponse and ReDisplacementCurrentResponse.

DonorConcentration.description: Donor doping concentration (ND) at each vertex, specified through the doping profile in the device structure. Used in physics models for computing carrier statistics, mobility, and recombination rates.

DopingDependence.description: Doping-dependent mobility model within Mobility(). The default Arora model computes mobility degradation due to ionized impurity scattering. Can also accept a PMI model identifier for custom doping-dependent mobility. Controls the low-field mobility as a function of doping concentration.

Electron.description: Electron continuity equation solved in Sentaurus Device. In the Solve section, specifies that the electron continuity equation is to be solved. In equation error control (Table 342), the reference value is 1e10 cm^-3 with relative error tolerance of 1e-5.

FowlerNordheim.description: Fowler-Nordheim tunneling current density (jFN) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset (unit: A/cm^2). Represents the field-emission tunneling current through thin insulating barriers. The tunneling model is activated within GateCurrent() using the Fowler keyword.

HighFieldSaturation.description: High-field saturation mobility model within Mobility() or eMobility()/hMobility(). Models the velocity saturation effect at high electric fields using Caughey-Thomas or TransferredElectronEffect formulations. The hole variant is hHighFieldSaturation.

Hole.description: Hole continuity equation solved in Sentaurus Device. In the Solve section, specifies that the hole continuity equation is to be solved. In equation error control (Table 342), the reference value is 1e10 cm^-3 with relative error tolerance of 1e-5.

Newton.description: Newton solver method for coupled equation systems. Specified through Coupled() in the Solve section, which solves all selected equations simultaneously using Newton-Raphson iteration. Supports relaxed Newton via AcceptNewtonParameter and controls convergence through the Math section error criteria.

Poisson.description: Poisson equation for electrostatic potential, solved as part of the coupled system. In Solve{} section, specifies Poisson as the equation to solve. Default error reference is 0.0258 V (thermal voltage at 300 K).

Potential.description: Electrostatic potential variable. In Aniso{}, enables anisotropic dielectric constant models. Also used as a reference in convergence criteria and error computation.

Radiative.description: Radiative recombination model within Recombination(). Models band-to-band radiative recombination using the optical recombination rate. Coefficient can be doping-dependent or constant.

SRH.description: Shockley-Read-Hall recombination model within Recombination(). Models trap-assisted recombination through defect energy levels in the bandgap. Supports doping dependence (dopant-dependent lifetimes), electric field dependence (Hurkx model), and temperature dependence.

SpaceCharge.description: Space charge density (rho) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Computed as the difference between positive and negative charge carriers including ions and free carriers. Units: cm^-3.

Stress.description: Mechanical stress model for strain-dependent simulations. Enables piezoresistive mobility effects and deformation potential effects on band structure. Used in Mechanics{} and Physics{} sections.

SurfaceSRH.description: Surface SRH recombination model within Recombination(). Models Shockley-Read-Hall recombination at material interfaces and surfaces with interface trap density and capture cross-section parameters.

TE.description: Polarization configuration option for LED optical simulations. Specifies transverse electric (TE) mode polarization in OpticalSolver{} or LED{} sections for far-field and near-field radiation pattern calculations.

TRBDF.description: Trapezoidal Rule / Backward Differentiation Formula (TR-BDF2) time integration method for transient simulations. Default transient method in Sentaurus Device. Combines the stability of BDF methods with the accuracy of trapezoidal integration.

TempDependence.description: Temperature dependence option within SRH(). Enables temperature-dependent carrier lifetimes for the SRH recombination model, accounting for the variation of trap-assisted recombination rates with lattice temperature.

Thermodynamic.description: Thermodynamic transport model that includes Peltier and Thomson effects in addition to Joule heating. Provides more accurate self-consistent electrothermal simulation by considering carrier energy flux and its coupling to the lattice thermal equation.

TotalCurrent.description: Total current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Computed as the sum of electron and hole current densities plus displacement current. Units: A/cm^2.

UCS.description: Unified Coordinate System for anisotropic model computations. Provides a consistent reference frame for evaluating tensor-valued material properties (mobility, permittivity) in anisotropic regions of the device.

eAvalanche.description: Electron impact ionization (avalanche) model within Avalanche() or Recombination(). Models carrier generation due to high-field impact ionization. Uses van Overstraeten-de Man, Chynoweth, or Selberherr models with user-definable coefficients.

eCurrent.description: Electron current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift-diffusion current of electrons. Units: A/cm^2.

eCurrentDensity.description: Electron current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in Plot{} and CurrentPlot{} sections. Represents the electron component of the total drift-diffusion current. Units: A/cm^2.

eDensity.description: Electron concentration (density) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the free electron carrier density computed from the solution of the electron continuity equation. Units: cm^-3.

eHighFieldSaturation.description: Electron high-field saturation velocity model within Mobility() or eMobility(). Configures the saturation velocity and high-field dependence of electron mobility using Caughey-Thomas or TransferredElectronEffect models.

eMobility.description: Electron mobility model within Mobility(). Configures all mobility sub-models (doping dependence, high-field saturation, etc.) specifically for electrons. Overrides generic Mobility() settings for the electron carrier type.

eQuasiFermi.description: Electron quasi-Fermi potential at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the electrochemical potential of electrons, used as a reference in DopingWell{} ramping and convergence monitoring. Units: eV.

eTemperature.description: Electron temperature at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in hydrodynamic or thermodynamic transport simulations. Used as a convergence criterion parameter and in DiffusionNoise{} temperature options. Units: K.

eVelocity.description: Electron velocity vector at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift velocity of electrons under the applied electric field. Units: cm/s.

hAvalanche.description: Hole impact ionization (avalanche) model within Avalanche() or Recombination(). Models hole-initiated carrier generation due to high-field impact ionization. Parallel to eAvalanche with separate coefficients.

hCurrent.description: Hole current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift-diffusion current of holes. Units: A/cm^2.

hCurrentDensity.description: Hole current density at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in Plot{} and CurrentPlot{} sections. Represents the hole component of the total drift-diffusion current. Units: A/cm^2.

hDensity.description: Hole concentration (density) at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the free hole carrier density computed from the solution of the hole continuity equation. Units: cm^-3.

hHighFieldSaturation.description: Hole high-field saturation velocity model within Mobility() or hMobility(). Configures the saturation velocity and high-field dependence of hole mobility using Caughey-Thomas or similar models.

hMobility.description: Hole mobility model within Mobility(). Configures all mobility sub-models (doping dependence, high-field saturation, etc.) specifically for holes. Overrides generic Mobility() settings for the hole carrier type.

hQuasiFermi.description: Hole quasi-Fermi potential at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the electrochemical potential of holes, used as a reference in DopingWell{} ramping and convergence monitoring. Units: eV.

hTemperature.description: Hole temperature at each vertex, available as a plot dataset in hydrodynamic or thermodynamic transport simulations. Used as a convergence criterion parameter and in DiffusionNoise{} temperature options. Units: K.

hVelocity.description: Hole velocity vector at each vertex, available as a plot dataset. Represents the drift velocity of holes under the applied electric field. Units: cm/s.

Optical.description: Optical physics model within Physics{}. Enables optical generation/recombination effects and couples the optical solver with the electrical simulation. Used in optoelectronic device simulations such as solar cells, LEDs, and photodetectors.
